per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: When I experience chest tightness and shortness of breath, I get a bit sweaty and feel a mild nausea, but it's not too severe. To be honest, it's been happening more frequently lately, especially after driving long hours on the road for work.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I get diaphoresis, or excessive sweating, during those episodes. It's usually accompanied by a sense of pressure in my chest and shortness of breath, which makes me feel really anxious.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yeah, it's really scary. Lately, my ankles have been feeling tighter than usual, and I've noticed some mild swelling there too. It's been happening for a few weeks now, but I'm not sure if it's related to anything else or just part of the same issue with chest tightness.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father actually passed away from a heart attack at the age of 52. It was a real shock for me and my whole family.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: It's one of the reasons I've been really anxious about these symptoms - my father's history with heart problems, and now me experiencing chest tightness and shortness of breath on exertion, makes me worry that I might be at risk too. It's a constant fear in the back of my mind, especially given my own hypertension diagnosis 8 years ago.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes, one thing that's been weighing on me a lot lately is my family history with heart disease. My mother has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's alive at 78, so I guess that's something to be grateful for. Still, it makes me worry about my own health even more when I think about my dad passing away from a heart attack at such a young age.
